summary: Single mean-reversion short proposal is structurally distinct, fully parseable against SUPPORTED_FIELDS, and projects to adequate sample size with broad exit-family compatibility.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Top pricechange winners (E126/E064/E066) are all LONG continuation entries keyed on taker-buy dominance; E148 is long with global_short_high. This proposal inverts the logic: SHORT side, taker-SELL dominance (taker_buy_ratio_5m<=0.40), positive funding (crowded-long fade), gated by move_pct>=3. The conjunction of (a) short-side, (b) taker-sell dominance, (c) positive-funding crowding, and (d) magnitude gate is not represented in the top-10 preview and is mechanically a mean-reversion fade rather than continuation — orthogonal to the dominant winning family. Across 94 existing shorts the slug is not a verbatim duplicate.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: pricechange base ~5000 events/30d × move_pct>=3 retention ~0.50 (pump events skew toward larger moves) = 2500 × funding>=0.0005 (positive & non-trivial, ~p60-p70 on alts) ~0.30 = 750 × taker_buy_ratio_5m<=0.40 (taker-sell dominant, roughly bottom ~30% of distribution) ~0.30 = ~225. Conservative; conditions are partially correlated (pumps with positive funding often still see net taker-buy, which would *reduce* the joint), so true count could land 120–250.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Mean-reversion fade with natural symmetric TP/SL favors `fixed` (the only exit family with positive mean/median in coverage; max 0.395) and `breakeven` (caps left tail, useful given R2 E126 asymmetric-TP/SL trap lesson). `time_only` is a poor fit (worst mean -0.73) since fades need price-based exits. `trail` is plausible if the reversion extends but historically negative-mean here. `multi_tp` weakest. Recommend prioritizing fixed (tight TP ~0.4–0.8%, SL ~0.6–1.2%) and breakeven variants; deprioritize time_only/multi_tp.
